generator: seed — the hourly Grok agent replaces this (see GROK-AGENT-PLAYBOOK.md)

stories.title: Lenox Township board convenes tomorrow with data center zoning on a crowded July agenda
stories.dek: The July 9 regular session lands as Macomb communities weigh moratoria and new standards for large-load projects.

stories.title: Consumers Energy formally abandons its 1.4 GW Lima Township gas plant after a Chelsea-area resident revolt
stories.dek: The utility canceled the 120-acre combustion project west of Ann Arbor; it maintains the Saline Stargate campus did not drive the pullout.

stories.title: Lansing posts public notice for a July 13 council hearing on a 182-day data center development pause
stories.dek: The Development and Planning Committee hearing is five days out; the pause follows Deep Green's withdrawn downtown proposal.

stories.title: Senate Democrats' seven-bill data center package remains the defining Lansing fight as committees stay quiet
stories.dek: SB 1046–1051 would cap water withdrawals, ban incentive NDAs, and require full-cost power tariffs; Republicans warn the guardrails could chase hyperscale investment.

stories.title: Saline Township trustees meet tonight as Stargate construction rolls on at the 1.4 GW Oracle-linked campus
stories.dek: The July 8 board session could surface construction updates; agendas do not guarantee a dedicated data center item.

stories.title: Texas Township's new 12-month data center moratorium is now in force, joining two neighboring Kalamazoo pauses
stories.dek: The July 5 effective date blocks new applications while the township drafts zoning standards for large-load facilities.

stories.title: Rural Washtenaw organizers celebrate after Consumers Energy drops a 1.4 GW gas plant that fueled grid-anxiety near Saline
stories.dek: Michigan Advance traces how Lima Township residents tied the combustion proposal to data-center load growth — and how the utility denied a direct link.

stories.title: Senate Democrats introduce an eight-bill package putting water caps, cost guarantees, and labor rules on data centers
stories.dek: SB 1046–1051 would cap water use at 2M gallons a day, make data centers pay full power costs, ban officials from signing NDAs, and require community benefits agreements. Gov. Whitmer endorsed the package.

stories.title: House Democrats follow with their own guardrail package — HB 6135–6142 adds a noise study to the mix
stories.dek: The House bills cover full-cost-of-service power, community benefits, water rules, an NDA ban tied to tax incentives, and decommissioning funds. Three more bills are expected.

stories.title: Bipartisan Capitol rally renews the push for a one-year statewide data center moratorium
stories.dek: Sen. Runestad and Rep. Wortz hosted residents and local officials on the Capitol lawn June 23. His SB 1018–1020 moratorium package sits in a committee unlikely to hear it.

stories.title: FOIA’d emails show Washington Township coordinated with Prologis for months on a withdrawn 800 MW campus
stories.dek: 1,642 pages of records fueled resident demands for transparency. Prologis pulled its application May 19; the township then passed a six-month moratorium.

stories.title: Altman and Whitmer mark construction at the $16B, 1.4 GW Stargate campus in Saline Township
stories.dek: Related Digital, Oracle, and OpenAI celebrated the 250-acre build June 1, announcing $22M in community investments and 2,500 union construction jobs.

stories.title: Saline Township voters rejected the Stargate project — construction began anyway under a consent judgment
stories.dek: A pre-existing legal settlement let the project proceed despite the referendum result, a case study in how binding township votes actually are.

stories.title: Google's 1 GW Van Buren Township project would fill 13.5 acres of wetlands, state filings show
stories.dek: The 'Project Cannoli' wetland permit is contested; the township approved its DTE substation in May after a five-hour meeting.

stories.title: Lawmakers move to end U-M's zoning exemption as its $1.2B Ypsilanti Township data center advances
stories.dek: Rep. Jimmie Wilson Jr. cites the university’s 124-acre purchase. The township opposes the project and the local water authority has a 12-month service moratorium.

stories.title: Oakland University trustees advance a campus data center to the due-diligence phase
stories.dek: The Fairmount Properties project near Lot 37 moves to environmental study before any final approval.

stories.title: Gaines Township tables Microsoft's data center rezoning after an hours-long public hearing
stories.dek: Microsoft offered binding commitments — a 65 dB noise cap, 150-ft setbacks, no groundwater use, no tax abatements. The request remains tabled with no new date.

stories.title: Recall petitions approved against three Lowell Township board members who voted down a data center moratorium
stories.dek: The Kent County Election Commission cleared the petitions as Microsoft eyes a five-building campus in the township.

stories.title: Solon Township tables the fifth draft of its data center zoning ordinance as an August moratorium deadline nears
stories.dek: Residents demanded stricter rules; an unnamed developer is interested in an 800+ acre site.

stories.title: Texas Township’s 12-month data center moratorium takes effect, joining Portage and Oshtemo
stories.dek: The pause took effect July 5 while the township writes zoning standards for large-load development.

stories.title: A Kalamazoo cannabis processor pitches its power-hungry grow facility to AI data center developers
stories.dek: Harbor Farmz is marketing 1 MW now — up to 15 MW in three years — for inference computing. The city says no formal proposal has been filed.

stories.title: Federal class action targets nonstop noise from the Dowagiac Bitcoin-mining campus now pivoting to AI
stories.dek: Residents compare the hum to living next to a highway that never sleeps. Hyperscale Data has since bought 48.5 more acres.

stories.title: Lansing moves toward a 182-day data center moratorium after Deep Green pulled its downtown proposal
stories.dek: The UK firm withdrew its 24 MW, $120M project April 6, just before a rezoning vote. A public hearing on the pause is set for July 13.

stories.title: Press association says Mason’s 60-day FOIA delays on data center records are illegal
stories.dek: The city cited a surge of data-center-driven records requests; the Michigan Press Association says the law does not allow that timeline.

stories.title: Saginaw’s six-month blanket prohibition on data centers nears its expiration window
stories.dek: Council adopted the unanimous pause January 13. What replaces it — new standards or an extension — is now the question.

stories.title: How easily could a hyperscale data center land in northern Michigan? Zoning gaps leave the door open
stories.dek: Interlochen Public Radio examines which northern townships have rules on the books — and which would be caught flat-footed.

stories.title: Data center rumors pack a UP township meeting — officials say no application has been filed
stories.dek: A speculative ~1,000-acre concept near the former K.I. Sawyer base drew an overflow crowd in West Branch Township. No permits, no named end users.

stories.title: Traverse City-area townships start writing data center rules before anyone asks to build
stories.dek: Garfield and East Bay townships are updating zoning now so they are not reacting to a live application later.

stories.title: The 1 GW Kalkaska proposal is dead — DNR refused state land and public opposition finished it
stories.dek: Rocklocker’s 1,440-acre concept collapsed in November, the north’s clearest signal yet that siting is not a formality.

meetings.topic: Regular meeting — Stargate construction updates possible
Regular meeting — data center zoning on the radar
Open meeting — oversees large-load electric agreements
